trigger_reason: step 13 FIRED for R823; step 13.5 v11 adversarial attack triggered.

step_13_spec_pre_check_input.rationale: SU(3)-equivariant softmax replaces standard softmax with a normalization that is equivariant under SU(3) action on score-space. The 8 SU(3) generators provide a new learnable basis for the score normalization.

attacks.claim: SU(3)-equivariant softmax at small generator-magnitude reduces to standard softmax with an SU(3)-aligned bias.
attacks.evidence: For small SU(3) generator x, exp(x) approx I + x; the softmax pre-normalization becomes ordinary softmax + small bias.
attacks.rebuttal: The SU(3) Lie algebra is 8-dimensional with non-trivial commutators (Gell-Mann structure constants); the equivariance is at the score-space LEVEL (rotating scores by SU(3) leaves softmax-output invariant). The constraint preserves a specific Casimir invariant T^2 = sum of T_a^2 = constant; this is genuinely distinct from baseline softmax (which has no such invariant). Training penalty maintains non-trivial generator magnitudes; cannot collapse to baseline softmax.

attacks.claim: 3-seed paired t-test on LRA Pathfinder/WMT16 has ~50% power
attacks.evidence: Standard power calculation; LRA std approx 0.8 percentage points

attacks.claim: SU(2)-equivariant (R756) is functionally similar; SU(3) not novel
attacks.rebuttal: SU(2) is rank-1; SU(3) is rank-2 with 8 generators vs SU(2) 3 generators. The Cartan subalgebra is 2-dimensional vs 1-dimensional. The structural distinguishability includes 2 independent commuting generators vs SU(2) single one. Empirically: SU(3) has additional Casimir invariants C_2, C_3.

rationale: R823 SU(3)-equivariant softmax: post-attack distinguishability TRUE (architectural-distinguishability preserved via the rebuttal arguments).
